picks.rationale: France enters this quarterfinal with a perfect 6-0 record in the 2026 World Cup, demonstrating dominant form with 17 goals scored and only 3 conceded. Historically, France holds a strong head-to-head advantage over Morocco, including a 2-0 victory in their most recent competitive meeting at the 2022 World Cup semi-final. Morocco is significantly hampered by the absence of their star forward Ismael Saibari due to injury, who was their top scorer in the group stage. Despite the absence of Tchouaméni, France's overall squad depth and offensive firepower, led by Mbappé, make them a clear favorite to win in regular time.

Public and sharp money are heavily backing the Under 2.5 goals, with the line moving from -120 to -115 (and even -125 in some markets), indicating a belief in a low-scoring affair. Morocco's defensive resilience has been a hallmark of their tournament run, and they will likely adopt a compact defensive strategy, especially without their key attacker Ismael Saibari. Quarterfinal matches often feature increased caution and tactical battles, leading to fewer goals. France can win this match without needing to score many goals, as seen in their 1-0 victory over Paraguay in the Round of 16.

Kylian Mbappé has been France's most prolific scorer in the tournament, with seven goals, and he is also the designated penalty taker. His central role in France's attack ensures a high volume of shots and scoring opportunities against any opponent. Even against a defensively strong Morocco, Mbappé's individual brilliance and ability to create chances make him a constant threat to find the back of the net. Mbappé is active and expected to start for France.

Ousmane Dembélé has been a significant offensive contributor for France, tallying four goals and two assists across five World Cup matches. With much of Morocco's defensive attention focused on containing Kylian Mbappé, Dembélé is likely to find more space and opportunities on the wing. His strong form, including 14 shots and five shots on goal in the tournament, provides good value at nearly 2-to-1 odds. Dembélé is an active player for France and expected to play a key role in the attack.

France's offensive prowess is undeniable, scoring at least three goals in all five of their prior World Cup matches, showcasing a consistently high-scoring attack. Their average of 2.83 goals per game in the tournament indicates a strong potential for multiple goals. While Morocco possesses a strong defense, France's attacking trio of Mbappé, Dembélé, and Olise is capable of breaking down even the most resolute backlines, making the Over 2.5 an attractive proposition at plus odds. The absence of Tchouaméni in midfield for France might also slightly open up the game, potentially leading to more end-to-end action.
